channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Trucordia Launches AI-Enabled Personal Lines Tool
item.description: Trucordia (Lindon, Utah) has launched Trucordia Go, an AI-enabled tool designed to help consumers explore auto and home insurance options. The result is fast and conversational, with optional support from licensed insurance professionals.” Trucordia says the tool can help consumers understand coverage options, identify coverage gaps and potential savings, and evaluate when a change may be needed. Trucordia Go will help empower a more informed and confident client to make the best insurance decisions for their personal needs.” Trucordia says the launch reflects its continued investment in technology to support the client experience and create a digital entry point to its licensed insurance professionals.

item.title: LexisNexis Launches Home Risk Location Model
item.description: LexisNexis Risk Solutions (Atlanta) has launched LexisNexis Location Intelligence for Home, an AI-driven predictive model designed to help U.S. The company says the capability combines industry-wide home claims data, location-based insights and predictive modeling to generate property-level risk scores. Location Intelligence for Home is available through LexisNexis Smart Selection, the company’s automated data service for inspection flags and customizable business rules.

item.title: Softening market tests underwriting discipline
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: The Week’s 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: Anduril Leads Varied Lineup Of Large Deals
item.description: Defense tech unicorn Anduril Industries led the fundraising lineup in a week heavy with rounds for companies focused on applications in the physical world. This is a weekly feature that runs down the week’s top 10 announced funding rounds in the U.S. Check out last week’s biggest funding deal roundup here .

item.title: Lemon apunta a operar sin terceros en Perú para mejorar costos y velocidad
item.description: La estrategia de construir con socios y luego aplicar a una licencia propia para acelerar productos y mejorar eficiencia operativa tiene un nuevo capítulo en Perú, donde la billetera fiat-cripto Lemon acaba de solicitar un permiso que perfila hacia dónde podrían moverse las billeteras digitales. Accede al análisis que impulsa la toma de decisiones en la banca latinoamericana Este contenido exclusivo está disponible solo para miembros registrados de iupana. Regístrate gratis o inicia sesión para leer el artículo completo y descubrir: Perspectivas estratégicas sobre innovación, regulación y tendencias en banca digital y fintech Entrevistas con líderes que están transformando el sector financiero en la región Reportes y estudios que anticipan los cambios clave del mercado Acceso prioritario a información para tomar decisiones informadas y fortalecer tu liderazgo Únete a la comunidad de ejecutivos que marcan la diferencia en la transformación digital de la banca.

item.title: Buenas Fuentes: Scotiabank Chile prepara BNPL y Bunq entra a México
item.description: Scotiabank Chile alista el lanzamiento de Buy Now, Pay Later , según su gerente país, Diego Masola. El neobanco holandés Bunq solicitó autorización para operar como banco en México , en una señal de la creciente competencia digital en el sistema financiero local. La firma apunta a atender a “ciudadanos globales” y suma a México a su estrategia de expansión, que también incluye Estados Unidos.

item.title: Life Insurers Face a New Longevity Reality
item.description: We’re facing a new longevity reality in America: people are living longer than ever before — a testament to many incredible advances in science, technology and healthcare — but at the same time, the gap between healthspan and lifespan is growing. John Hancock ’s (Boston) Longevity Preparedness Index (LPI) —created in collaboration with the MIT AgeLab—offers a first-of-its-kind benchmark for how ready American adults are to live well in older age across eight key areas: social connection, finance, daily activities, care, home, community, health, and life transitions. As a result, we face an urgent question: are we truly prepared to live longer?

item.title: Ivans, Cytora Target Commercial Submission Friction
item.description: For decades, Ivans (Tampa, Fla.) has occupied a largely invisible but essential place in the insurance industry’s infrastructure. Applied Systems (Chicago) acquired the property/casualty business of Ivans in 2013. “Others have used the phrase ‘plumbing’ or ‘connectivity layer’ or ‘infrastructure utility,’” comments Michael Streit, President, Ivans.

item.title: Applied’s Graham Blackwell Sees AI Accelerating Connected Insurance
item.description: Source: Applied Systems.) Applied Systems (Chicago) CEO-designate Graham Blackwell says the company’s next phase will focus on accelerating what it calls the “Digital Roundtrip of Insurance,” using AI and workflow automation to connect agencies, carriers and wholesalers more tightly across the insurance lifecycle. (Graham Blackwell, who will assume the CEO role for Applied Systems later this year. Graham Blackwell, President and CEO-designate, Applied Systems.

item.title: Judge Considers Anthropic’s $1.5 Billion Settlement of Authors’ Lawsuit
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday pressed lawyers for more information on several points of artificial intelligence company Anthropic’s proposed $1.5 billion settlement with authors who accused it of misusing their books to train its AI chatbot Claude. Related: OpenAI Floats Idea of Global AI Governance Body With US, China Authors and other copyright holders filed claims covering over 92% of the more than 480,000 works included in the settlement, an attorney for the authors said during the hearing. The settlement has spurred objections from authors who have argued it is not large enough, overcompensates the plaintiffs’ attorneys or wrongly excludes some copyright owners.

item.title: FCA, Bank of England and Treasury joint statement on frontier AI models and cyber resilience
item.description: Frontier AI models represent a step-change in capability, with significant implications for cyber security and operational resilience. The cyber capabilities of current frontier AI models are already exceeding what a skilled practitioner could achieve, and at a significantly higher speed, greater scale, and lower cost. Firms should read and consider the effective practices on cyber resilience published by the Bank, PRA and FCA in October 2025.

item.title: Integral ILS encouraged by AuM growth in larger, more sophisticated market: Lowther and Sannemalm
item.description: Artemis recently met with Richard Lowther, Co-Founder and Managing Partner and Kristofer Sannemalm, Head of Underwriting for Integral ILS, at their office in Bermuda, to discuss market trends and Integral’s approach as the manager’s assets under management sits at a healthy $3.5 billion as of January 2026. Today, Lowther feels that the asset class has evolved with more sophistication at the broker level in matching risk to various ILS market appetites, as well as consultants having a better understanding of the unique attributes of ILS platforms to guide their client’s investment strategies. Expanding on this, Sannemalm emphasised that the structural maturity of the asset through its strong track record and industry adopted catastrophe risk models means that today, “the due diligence process is more streamlined and comprehensive to better highlight the bespoke advantages each manager brings to the table, and whether they align with a client’s investment philosophy.” Over the years, transparency has improved in the ILS space, but while it’s important to work with investors to give them what they want, Lowther stressed that tailoring based on individual client needs is critical as one size of reporting does not necessarily fit all.

item.title: Pro Global strengthens LatAm offering to support growth and a complex insurance market
item.description: Pro Global, a specialist adviser to the specialist insurance sector, has strategically strengthened its offering in Latin America to support growth and increasing operational complexity in the insurance market. “These services, which Pro Global already delivers in the Latin American market, form part of an integrated proposition that complements its established capabilities in claims management, consulting, digital transformation, underwriting support, and operational services. Together, they position Pro Global as a strategic partner capable of supporting clients across the entire insurance value chain, in both local operations and increasingly complex international environments,” Pro Global stated.

item.title: Evolving geopolitical landscape is creating new opportunities in the Middle East: Pelagos CEO
item.description: The evolving geopolitical landscape in the Middle East is opening new opportunities in the region, Dan Burrows, Pelagos Insurance Capital CEO, commented during the company’s recent earnings call. The evolving geopolitical landscape is creating new opportunities in this region, which we are well positioned to continue executing on.” The executive pointed to the company’s rapid operational response as a key differentiator following the initial escalation of tensions in the region. Demonstrating our ability to not only match the right capital to the right risk, but also to the right partner at the right time.” Burrows also commented on how the geopolitical events in the Middle East impacted this quarter, highlighting that the financial tailwinds from these accounts will manifest more later in the year.

item.title: Centre for Disaster Protection’s new platform to help accelerate risk financing solutions
item.description: The Centre for Disaster Protection (the Centre) has launched Labs, a new innovation platform designed to accelerate practical solutions that help governments better plan, finance, and respond to disasters. This means prioritising solutions for the scale and complexity of modern disasters; working collaboratively and sharing knowledge openly; and developing tools rooted in real policy and operational needs to accelerate effective and scalable pre-arranged disaster risk financing. Initial areas of exploration include tools to help governments quantify financial exposure to disasters, design effective parametric triggers for disaster financing, and compare options for structuring disaster risk financing instruments.

item.title: Allianz’s PIMCO, L&G Sued in Scrap Over €1.2 Billion Brussels Tower
item.description: PIMCO and Legal & General Group Plc have been accused of placing “undue pressure” on property valuers in a bitter scrap over the future of a famous Brussels skyscraper whose owner has collapsed into insolvency. The lawyers allege the lenders placed “undue pressure” on valuers at Knight Frank LLP “to reduce its valuation to a figure below €950 million ($1.1 billion).” Large office buildings like the Finance Tower don’t change hands often, so there can be disagreement over true value. The owner of The Finance Tower, the 142 meter (466 feet) high building located in downtown Brussels, filed a lawsuit in the English High Court in a bid to to stall an attempt by its lenders to freeze rental income, according to documents lodged in London and New York courts.

item.title: Oil Tankers Transiting Strait of Hormuz Since Start of Iran War
item.description: Here’s a look at some of the non-Iranian oil tankers that have passed through the strait since the start of the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran on February 28, listed by cargo destination, according to data from LSEG and Kpler. has maintained a blockade of Iranian ports, while Iran lifted and then reimposed its own blockade on marine traffic passing through the Strait of Hormuz, which typically handles roughly one-fifth of the world’s oil supply. Agios Fanourios I, a VLCC tanker loaded with Iraqi crude and bound for Vietnam crossed the Strait of Hormuz through Iran’s designated route on May 10, Iran’s semi-official Tasnim news agency said.

item.title: Walmart to Pay $230K in Illinois Disability Hiring Discrimination Settlement
item.description: Walmart agreed to pay $230,000 and furnish other relief to settle a disability hiring discrimination lawsuit filed by the U.S. According to the EEOC’s suit, a deaf applicant applied online for a stocking position at a Walmart in Decatur, Illinois. When an associate responsible for screening applicants from Walmart reached out to him to set up an interview, the deaf applicant requested an American Sign Language (ASL) interpreter.

item.title: Sixfold launches underwriting AI solution on Microsoft Marketplace
item.description: Sixfold has announced that its underwriting AI platform is now available through Microsoft Marketplace, allowing insurers to access and deploy the company’s AI capabilities directly within their existing Microsoft Azure environments. According to Sixfold, the Microsoft Marketplace integration is intended to support broader adoption of AI within underwriting operations by simplifying access to the technology inside insurers’ existing cloud ecosystems. By making the platform available through Microsoft Marketplace, carriers can align deployment with existing Azure commitments, cloud budgets and enterprise procurement processes.

item.title: Exzeo launches WindForm Pro to support Florida wind mitigation compliance
item.description: Exzeo has introduced WindForm Pro, a new AI-driven solution designed to help Florida insurers comply with updated wind mitigation inspection requirements issued by the Florida Office of Insurance Regulation (OIR). The company said the platform is designed to reduce administrative workload, improve data consistency and support faster compliance reporting by digitizing and standardizing wind mitigation workflows. The product became available to Florida carriers on May 5 and is intended to automate the processing of wind mitigation inspection forms, which insurers use to assess property resilience and determine eligibility for premium discounts.

item.title: ManageMy integrates with Snowflake to expand insurer data and reporting capabilities
item.description: The post ManageMy integrates with Snowflake to expand insurer data and reporting capabilities appeared first on Insurtech Insights . ManageMy has announced a new integration with Snowflake designed to provide insurance carriers with direct access to operational data and reporting across policy, billing, claims and distribution workflows. The integration connects ManageMy’s insurance platform with Snowflake’s AI Data Cloud infrastructure, enabling carriers to access near real-time reporting, self-service analytics and broader enterprise data connectivity without relying heavily on IT or vendor support.

item.title: Zurich appoints Northern regional market head
item.description: Zurich UK appointed Andy King as Head of Regional Market for the North as the insurer continues to invest in regional growth, broker relationships, and service capabilities. King, who has spent nearly 16 years at Zurich in roles spanning claims, operations, and broker engagement, will lead the Northern region with a broker focused engagement strategy tailored to individual partner needs. The insurer said its Mid Market business has seen strong new business growth this year and plans to hire 15 additional underwriters in the coming months to support demand and maintain service standards.

item.title: Sun Life launches workplace savings plans for Canadian SMEs
item.description: Sun Life Financial introduced Sun Life Essentials and Sun Life Essentials Plus, digital-first workplace savings plans designed for small and medium-sized businesses in Canada. The plans support RRSP and TFSA savings options, include no setup fees or minimum contribution requirements, and are built around Sun Life Global Investments’ Granite target date funds. The products are designed to simplify workplace savings adoption for SMEs while helping employees improve long-term financial security.

item.title: Stand launches catastrophe risk model
item.description: The model combines physics simulations with AI inference to evaluate catastrophe risk and identify mitigation steps that could improve a property’s survivability. Stand uses the model to support underwriting and mitigation recommendations for catastrophe-exposed homes. Stand Insurance Solutions announced the launch of the Stand World Model , a physics-based AI model designed to simulate how homes respond to wildfire, flood, wind, and earthquakes at the structure level.

item.title: For Carriers, AI Can Now Mean Hyper-Personalized Customer Service, Leaders Say
item.description: He and others stressed that rapidly evolving technology, including artificial intelligence systems, while far from perfect, have positioned insurers to focus more on customer service, anticipating policyholder needs in unprecedented ways and providing personalized attention like never before. But one advantage of the tech means that carriers can hone customer service in ways undreamt of just a few years ago. “You want your customer personalization to really come across as a concierge service,” said Russell Page, chief information officer for Hagerty, an insurer that specializes in coverage for classic cars and specialty vehicles.

item.title: The Race for California Insurance Commissioner: Meet Steve Bradford
item.description: This article is part of a multi-part series introducing candidates who are running for the position of insurance commissioner in California. Democrat Steve Bradford, an education organization board member, previously served in the California State Senate, the state Assembly and on the Gardena City Council. His plans include addressing transparency in insurance pricing, creating rewards for risk mitigation, addressing insurance availability and fixing the FAIR Plan.

item.title: Who Will Be California’s Next Insurance Commissioner?
item.description: Related: California Commissioner Advances Proposal to Overhaul Intervenor Process Incumbent Democratic Insurance Commissioner Ricardo Lara, who is term-limited after serving two terms, has not made known any next steps in his political career. The race for the California Insurance Commissioner post has drawn 11 candidates covering several parties, and backgrounds that include two insurance agents, a former broker, a state legislator and a former state legislator. Addressing the state’s “insurance crisis,” which took on a bigger priority following the devastating January 2025 Los Angeles Wildfires, is a loud message coming from most of the field.

item.title: FLASH Weather AI Launches Hail Prediction Model
item.description: FLASH Weather AI has launched a new high-resolution hail prediction model that can give almost an hour of warning before a storm. FLASH’s 1-Hour Hail Swath Prediction Model is a deep-learning system that forecasts hail size and arrival time at 1-kilometer resolution, refreshed every five minutes, with predictions extending up to 55 minutes into the future. FLASH said that after a month of beta testing across multiple severe weather events, the model has demonstrated significant accuracy and operational usability, with verification examples available on request.

item.title: Howden, Ardonagh, Antares, Swiss Re, Sedgwick: 12 APAC insurance people moves of the week
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: 5 Interesting Startup Deals You May Have Missed: A Law Firm Operating System, Building Defense Tech Near The Battlefield, And Cell-Based Milk
item.description: They include a defense-tech startup that aims to bring manufacturing closer to the frontlines, a company working to recycle valuable raw materials from defunct solar panels at industrial scale, and a startup that wants to produce cell-based milk for the dairy supply chain. This is a monthly column that runs down five interesting startup funding deals that may have flown under the radar. A decade ago, defense tech was considered a niche and sometimes controversial corner of venture capital, with few startup investors daring to place bets on companies working with the military.

item.title: Settled catastrophe bond issuance now in double-digit billions for 2026, pipeline robust
item.description: Catastrophe bond issuances that have been fully settled in the market now amount to more than $10.4 billion, while the pipeline for new cat bond deals remains robust and Artemis Deal Directory data already forecasts around $14.35 billion of settled issuance by early June. Catastrophe bond issuance, across full Rule 144A and private deals we’ve tracked in 2026 is currently keeping pace with the record amount seen last year. There is a way to go before getting excited about potential new half-year issuance records, but whether those are broken or not 2026 continues the cat bond markets trend of robust activity levels.

item.title: Rochdale sees elevated industry-loss warranty (ILW) demand as mutual fund returns 11.27%
item.description: The City National Rochdale Select Strategies Fund delivered a net return of 11.27% for the year ended January 31st 2026 and its investment adviser said demand for industry-loss warranty (ILW) protection has been elevated, leading it to expect promising opportunities for 2026 portfolio construction. We expect to see both the losses from once “non-peak” perils and the consequent demand for protection continue to rise as exposure growth and climate conditions lead to more property at risk and higher replacement costs for that property.” Looking ahead, he also commented on prospects for the next year, saying, “The Fund’s portfolio construction process for 2026 is now well underway, and we are focused on diversification and capital-efficient opportunities in the industry loss warranty (“ILW”) and catastrophe bond (“Cat Bond”) markets, where we believe risk-adjusted returns are most attractive. The City National Rochdale Select Strategies Fund is a mutual insurance-linked securities (ILS) strategy focused on industry loss warranties (ILWs) and catastrophe bonds, that invests the majority of its assets into NB RE Ltd., a Bermuda based structure whose origination and management falls to global asset manager Neuberger Berman’s specialist insurance-linked securities (ILS) investment team.

item.title: AM Best upgrades MS Reinsurance’s issuer credit rating
item.description: AM Best has upgraded MS Amlin AG’s (MS Reinsurance) (Switzerland) Long-Term Issuer Credit Rating (Long-Term ICR) to “aa” (Superior) from “aa-” (Superior) and affirmed its Financial Strength Rating (FSR) of A+ (Superior). The Long-Term ICR upgrade reflects the assignment of full rating enhancement to MS Reinsurance due to its increased strategic and financial importance to parent company MSI. According to AM Best, MSI’s ratings are being extended to MS Reinsurance given the implicit and explicit support provided, including a full unconditional parental guarantee.

item.title: Willis and Cornell University collaborate on research into correlated catastrophe risk
item.description: By combining Cornell’s cutting edge research with Willis’s deep industry experience, we can give our clients clearer visibility into evolving catastrophe risks and support more resilient decision making.” From Cornell University, Prof. Our partners at Cornell will help us determine the true risk of correlated catastrophes in the present day and get prepared for the possibility of greater synchrony in the near future.” Cameron Rye, Director of Natural Catastrophe Analytics for Willis Re, said: “Understanding how multiple natural perils may align in a warming world is becoming increasingly important for reinsurers, who have traditionally modelled the risk from different perils independently. Willis, a global insurance broker and risk advisory firm, has announced a new scientific collaboration with researchers at Cornell University and the Atkinson Center for a Sustainable Future.

item.title: Mercury Insurance invests in BurnBot to advance wildfire mitigation
item.description: Mercury Insurance, a California-based provider of auto and home insurance, has made a strategic investment in BurnBot to advance wildfire mitigation and support more affordable and accessible insurance coverage. Working with BurnBot, we are increasing our understanding of how on-the-ground risk reduction translates into expanded insurance availability and improved affordability for homeowners and communities.” Anukool Lakhina, CEO of BurnBot, added, “Mercury’s leadership in leaning into mitigation is important for the future of wildfire insurance in California and beyond. “We are excited to support Mercury in building practical pathways that connect real-world risk reduction with insurance outcomes.” The partnership will initially focus on California, where BurnBot’s mitigation efforts are already underway, potentially expanding over time based on results and learnings.

item.title: Finland’s First Drone Scare Near Helsinki Disrupts Flights
item.description: Finland warned the public of drones possibly entering its airspace, for the first time near Helsinki, in the early hours of Friday. Flights were disrupted at the country’s biggest airport, Helsinki-Vantaa, and about 1.8 million residents of southern Finland were told to remain indoors after overnight Ukrainian strikes on Russia near Finland prompted concern that unmanned aircraft had strayed into the country. The northernmost region they mentioned in the drone report was Pskov, which borders Estonia and Latvia, but not Finland.

item.title: NH House Passes Self-Insurance for Childcare, Behavioral Health Businesses
item.description: Senate Bill 614 establishes “multiple-caregiver self-insured risk coverage arrangements” for childcare, day care, foster care, and behavioral health services programs and providers. On the last day to vote on bills, the New Hampshire House of Representatives passed a bill aimed at reducing liability insurance costs for care professionals. The bill would create a formal agreement between two or more of these providers, managed by the Department of Insurance, in which each business would pay into a fund that could be jointly used for self-insurance against liability risks, joint insurance purchases, legal fees, and more.

item.title: Georgia Insurance Agent Kept Comp Premiums, Gave Fake COIs, Board Says
item.description: A Georgia insurance agent whose license expired a year ago has been charged with diverting workers’ compensation premiums from businesses and providing falsified certificates of insurance. Suarez, owner of EliteOne Solutions in Dalton, is charged with insurance fraud and forgery, the state Board of Workers’ Compensation told news outlets . The board’s investigation found that the alleged premium theft left clients without comp coverage.

item.title: Just Insure seeks $4 million in convertible notes
item.description: Just Insure disclosed a $4 million convertible note financing, according to a newly filed Form D with the SEC. On the capital side, parent SF Insuretech completed a $4.975 million convertible note financing in early 2025 and an $8 million bridge round in February 2026 to address ongoing going concern pressures. The Los Angeles-based insurer reported selling $1.4 million of the offering to four investors, with $2.6 million remaining.

item.title: Exzeo launches WindForm Pro
item.description: Exzeo launched WindForm Pro, an AI-powered solution designed to help Florida insurers comply with updated wind mitigation inspection requirements issued by the Florida Office of Insurance Regulation. WindForm Pro is part of Exzeo’s broader Insurance-as-a-Service platform for P&C insurers, which includes tools for underwriting, policy administration, claims, analytics, and financial reporting. According to Exzeo, the latest changes to Florida’s wind mitigation requirements have increased documentation and data capture demands, creating operational challenges for insurers still relying on manual workflows and fragmented systems.

item.title: Go.Compare launches ChatGPT app for insurance comparison
item.description: UK comparison site Go.Compare launched a ChatGPT app that allows consumers to compare car, van, and home insurance products directly within ChatGPT. The integration enables users to access Go.Compare’s comparison services without leaving the chat interface. “Integrating Go.Compare with ChatGPT is an exciting step into AI and provides a new way for customers to find our service.

item.title: El Consejo General de Mediadores reclama equilibrio entre carga regulatoria e interlocución con aseguradoras
item.description: El presidente del Consejo General de Mediadores de Seguros, Javier Barberá, reclamó ayer un mayor diálogo entre corredores y compañías aseguradoras y alertó de que el incremento de obligaciones regulatorias no va acompañado de las condiciones necesarias para ejercer la profesión en igualdad de condiciones . El presidente del Consejo General situó estas tensiones en un contexto de concentración creciente en el mercado asegurador. En esa línea, rechazó cualquier modelo basado en la confrontación entre Mediación y aseguradoras.

item.title: Coseba reafirma su objetivo de triplicar el negocio en 2030
item.description: José Clavellinas, presidente ejecutivo y socio fundador, expuso e l plan estratégico denominado hasta 2030, cuyo objetivo central es triplicar el volumen de negocio registrado a cierre de 2023 . Coseba 1986 celebró del 6 al 10 de mayo su XVII Convención Premier en Agadir (Marruecos). El encuentro reunió a los miembros de su Red Premier y coincidió con el 20.º aniversario de esa red y el 40.º aniversario de la correduría cordobesa.

item.title: La población española suspende en cultura aseguradora
item.description: Un estudio elaborado por YouGov para Mapfr e revela que la comprensión de los términos habituales en los contratos de seguro es limitada entre la población española. La población cuenta con una media de 2,6 seguros. Es decir, existe un segmento de población que mantiene coberturas activas sin verificar periódicamente sus condiciones, renovaciones o exclusiones.

item.title: NCCI: Workers’ Comp Calendar Year Combined Ratio at 91; Accident Year CR 102
item.description: NCCI reported that while the calendar year 2025 combined ratio for workers’ compensation is 91 (compared with 86 the prior year), the workers’ comp accident year 2025 combined ratio is 102 with prior years continuing to experience downward reserve development. She said the rise in 2025 accident year combined ratio is not surprising, adding that California, which accounts for 20% of the workers’ comp industry, reported a 2025 accident year combined ratio of 129. NCCI said that it’s the 12th consecutive year where the workers’ comp calendar year measured below 100, an indication of the line’s overall profitability.

item.title: Adapting Claim Investigations for AI-Driven Fraud
item.description: Admiral, one of the United Kingdom’s largest motor and home insurers, reported a 71% year-over-year increase in fraud in 2025, driven in part by AI-generated evidence. Deepfakes were the phenomenon of 2024, but the synthetic claim has now quickly become the defining fraud trend. Professionals must completely change how they approach claim investigations and decisions.

item.title: Report: Hybrid Vehicle Collision Claims Hit Record High in First Quarter
item.description: The report also shows that in the first quarter, the share of repairable collision claims for battery electric vehicles (BEVs) held steady at 3.33% in the U.S. and 5.28% in Canada for the first quarter, according to Mitchell’s its Q1 2026 Plugged-In: EV Collision Insights report. Claims for mild hybrid electric vehicles surged to record highs in North America, with year-over-year increases of 25% in the U.S.

item.title: Delta Insurance New Zealand hires Petr Dorofeev as tech, cyber product lead
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: ‘Pivotal moment’: Lloyd’s chief says no need for subtlety in soft cycle fight
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Marsh to acquire Japanese energy giant Eneos’ insurance units
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles
